title: Network Path Change Detection
spl:
`path_viz_index` thousandeyes.test.type="agent-to-server"
| stats dc(path_hash) as unique_paths count by thousandeyes.source.agent.name, server.address
| where unique_paths > 1
| sort -unique_paths
description: Detects when the network path between an agent and a target changes, which can indicate routing instability, ISP re-routing, or failover events. Correlating path changes with latency spikes helps isolate root cause.
value: Detects when the network path between an agent and a target changes, which can indicate routing instability, ISP re-routing, or failover events. Correlating path changes with latency spikes helps isolate root cause.
implementation: Path Visualization data must be enabled in the Tests Stream input. This use case requires building a path fingerprint (hash of intermediate hops) over time windows to detect when routes shift. Correlate with `network.latency` from the metrics stream to identify performance-impacting path changes.
visualization: Timeline (path changes over time), Table (agent, server, unique paths), Drilldown to ThousandEyes via `thousandeyes.permalink`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719).
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes Path Visualization data.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Path Visualization data must be enabled in the Tests Stream input. This use case requires building a path fingerprint (hash of intermediate hops) over time windows to detect when routes shift. Correlate with `network.latency` from the metrics stream to identify performance-impacting path changes.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`path_viz_index` thousandeyes.test.type="agent-to-server"
| stats dc(path_hash) as unique_paths count by thousandeyes.source.agent.name, server.address
| where unique_paths > 1
| sort -unique_paths
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Path Change Detection** ‚ÄĒ Detects when the network path between an agent and a target changes, which can indicate routing instability, ISP re-routing, or failover events. Correlating path changes with latency spikes helps isolate root cause.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes Path Visualization data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Invokes macro `path_viz_index` ‚ÄĒ in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by thousandeyes.source.agent.name, server.address** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where unique_paths > 1` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚ÄĘ Orders rows with `sort` ‚ÄĒ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Compare the same tests and time window in the Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk dashboard or the test view at app.thousandeyes.com so Splunk‚Äôs metrics and states match the vendor. If they disagree, check streaming or HEC inputs, macros, and API or token health before retuning.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (path changes over time), Table (agent, server, unique paths), Drilldown to ThousandEyes via `thousandeyes.permalink`.
grandmaExplanation: We watch when the path between two points flips, so we can tell a reroute that hurt performance from a small blip.
knownFalsePositives: Paths shift during normal ISP and cloud reroutes; some changes add hops without hurting latency or loss.
